LEMON CURD : LEMON CURD WITH THYME (VARIATION)

CLASSIC LEMON CURD

Ingredients:
- 6 egg yolks
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup fresh lemon juice
- 2 tablespoons lemon zest
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 6 tablespoons butter, cubed
- 1/4 teaspoon vanilla extract

Instructions for Classic:
Whisk together egg yolks and sugar in heat-safe bowl.
Add lemon juice, zest, and salt.
Set bowl over pot of simmering water (double boiler).
Whisk constantly for 12-15 minutes until thickened.
Temperature should reach 160°F for food safety.
Remove from heat.
Whisk in butter cubes one at a time until melted and smooth.
Add vanilla extract.
Strain through fine sieve to remove any cooked egg bits.
Transfer to sterilized jars.
Cool to room temperature before refrigerating.

---

VARIATION: LEMON CURD WITH THYME

Ingredients (same as above, plus):
- 2 tablespoons fresh thyme leaves (or 1 tablespoon dried)
- 1/4 teaspoon ground white pepper (optional)

Instructions for Thyme Variation:
Follow same procedure as classic recipe.
After whisking in butter, add fresh thyme leaves and white pepper.
Let steep for 2-3 minutes.
Strain through fine sieve, pressing gently on thyme to extract flavor.
Transfer to jars and cool.
The thyme adds subtle herbal notes that complement the citrus.

Storage:
Refrigerate up to 4 weeks in airtight jars.
Or freeze up to 6 months.

Uses for Lemon Curd:
- Spread on toast or English muffins
- Fill tart shells with cream and berries
- Swirl into yogurt or whipped cream
- Pipe into small tarts or pastry shells
- Layer in parfait with meringue pieces
- Top vanilla ice cream
- Mix into pound cake batter

Prep time: 10 minutes
Cooking time: 20 minutes
Cooling time: 30 minutes
Yields: about 1 1/2 cups
